reason: Frontend spec entry — lists all conventions; this task lives in web/ frontend layer.
Feature-first layout; this task adds web/src/features/weak-passwords/{components,hooks,api,routes} including the highly-sensitive OneTimePasswordDialog and CleartextWindowBanner.

Stateful render branches (window pending/open/expired), explicit unauthorized vs empty vs error separation, masking via <MaskedCell />, no-color-only state semantics. Critical for window banner three-state and auditor unauthorized rendering.
Domain hook naming (use-weak-password-reports / use-cleartext-window / use-export-weak-password-cleartext); polling-stop / mutation reset conventions.
Sensitive temporary state rules — single most important spec for this task. oneTimePassword limited to dialog useState + mutation result; no localStorage / sessionStorage / URL / global / cache.
Branded ID aliases for ReportId/TaskId/ExportId; runtime zod parse with passwordStored: z.literal(false) preserved; forbidden any.
UI forbidden patterns — sensitive fields rendered as plain strings, missing unauthorized vs disabled distinction, missing window-state banner, sensitive temp state persistence — all relevant here.
Frontend mirrors backend zod contracts; weak-password mirror must preserve passwordStored: z.literal(false), oneTimePassword field, SENSITIVE_EXPORT_EXPIRED error code parity.
Reuse <StatusBadge>/<UnauthorizedState>/<EmptyState>/<ErrorState>/<MaskedCell /> from console + vuln + mail + log; do NOT duplicate window-state literals or oneTimePassword handling.
Platform PRD §7.5 weak password (30-min window, no plaintext storage, one-time XLSX password, audit must not contain plaintext, LLM no-touch); §3 viewer/auditor permission boundaries.
Parent MVP PRD: wave order, 30-min window global constraint, frontend stack constraints.
Tracker parent PRD: shared contract mirror policy, URL state spec, sensitive display rules, role gating, suggested implementation order.
Sibling task that builds App / router / Providers / MSW worker / fetch helper / ActorProvider; this task depends on its shells and reuses its shared components.
Sibling task that established the 4-domain shared list pattern (URL state, viewer route guard, MSW handler-side query/grouping).
Sibling task that established the explicit-edge-case banner pattern; weak-password top status banner adopts the same pattern.
Sibling task that introduces shared <MaskedCell /> component used here for password masked rendering.
Backend follow-up that fills the cleartext-view contract gap; this frontend task's reveal placeholder button references it.
This task's own PRD with locked decisions, pages, hooks, MSW handlers, ADR-lite, R1–R13, AC, sensitive display hard constraints table.

Source of truth for weakPasswordMaskedFindingSchema, weakPasswordCleartextFindingSchema, exportWeakPasswordCleartextCommandSchema, reportExportResultSchema (oneTimePassword, contentPreview), reportExportRecordSchema (passwordStored: z.literal(false), expiresAt); web mirror copies field names/enums verbatim.
ReportQuery defines filter dimensions for the masked findings list endpoint (assetGroupId, reportType=WEAK_PASSWORD, taskId, limit).
Authoritative implementation of 30-min window enforcement (WEAK_PASSWORD_CLEAR_WINDOW_MS) and SENSITIVE_EXPORT_EXPIRED error path; the MSW mock handler must mirror this exactly.

permissionPoints (weak_password:cleartext_view, weak_password:cleartext_export, report:export), domainErrorCodes (SENSITIVE_EXPORT_EXPIRED) consumed by gating, dialog error branch, and audit-tip rendering.
API error envelope shape that weak-password MSW handlers emit; SENSITIVE_EXPORT_EXPIRED triggers dialog red expired state.
ActorContext / permissionPoints consumed by route-level guard, export button gating, auditor unauthorized rendering.
TaskRecord.lifecycleStage and terminal-state timestamps used by use-task-completion hook to derive cleartext window expiresAt baseline.
